ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-26-2024 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-26-2024 Continued To May-21-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-26-2024 Set For Order To Show Cause On Apr-16-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

The City and County of San Francisco's demurrers to the first amended complaint (FAC) are sustained.

Plaintiff filed a government claim alleging a May 2022 slip-and-fall on a sidewalk at 2061 Clement Street - a claim the City denied. Plaintiff's FAC, however, pleads a fall on a "negligently maintained sidewalk" some five blocks away "at or near 2601 Clement St."

The City first demurs as to plaintiff's failure to meet the requirement that a government claim state the "place" of the alleged occurrence. (Govt. Code 910(c).) Plaintiff concedes the discrepancy between her government claim and her complaint, but argues that the former properly "listed the 'place' of the incident" as "on or near Clement Street." (Opp. 2:3-8.) The statute's plain language does not support reading "place" as a complete many-blocks-long street. Such an interpretation would deprive the City of the notice needed to investigate and decide whether to settle claims before litigation, as well as to be alerted to the need for repairs, if any. (See City of San Jose v. Sup. Ct. (1974) 12 Cal.3d 447, 455.)

The City also demurs to the FAC's general negligence claim as not authorized by statute - a point plaintiff's opposition concedes by silence.

Because the FAC's flaws cannot be remedied, the demurrers are sustained without leave to amend.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
